topic: Someone finds out their best friend is moving across the country
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt heavy in Maya’s hands, the thick cardstock a tangible representation of the news. She reread the address three times, a slow, dizzying smile spreading across her face until her cheeks ached. Liam was moving to Seattle. Seattle! The thought sent a jolt of pure, unadulterated joy through her chest, making her take a sharp, involuntary breath. She bounced on the balls of her feet, nearly tripping over the rug. "No way," she whispered to the empty room, her voice thick with disbelief and delight. She snatched her phone, already planning elaborate farewell celebrations, her fingers flying across the screen as she started drafting a ridiculously over-the-top welcome-back party itinerary. This was it; the start of an adventure she could only dream about.

[story 2]

When Chloe finally pieced together the details—the lease agreement, the moving truck schedule—a bright, bubbling feeling erupted in her core. She let out a loud, unrestrained laugh, a sound so free it startled the cat. "He's actually doing it!" she exclaimed, spinning around in her small apartment, her energy practically vibrating off her skin. Her eyes shone, wide and sparkling as she took in the reality of the situation. A wave of warmth washed over her, settling deep in her bones, making her feel lighter than air. She immediately started pacing the length of her living room, tapping her foot in a quick, excited rhythm, already imagining the sprawling landscapes of the Pacific Northwest and all the new experiences waiting for them.

[story 3]

Mark stared at the printed confirmation email, his initial shock dissolving into a rush of exhilarating freedom. A wide grin stretched across his face, pulling at the corners of his eyes. He dropped the paper onto the desk, the sound barely registering over the rapid thumping in his own ears. "Across the country? Seriously?" he managed to stammer out, his voice high with excitement. He pushed back from his chair, taking a deep, expansive breath that seemed to fill his entire lungs. A powerful sense of possibility washed over him, making him want to run—to sprint down the street, to shout the news to the nearest person. Every nerve ending felt alight, buzzing with an intense, almost physical eagerness for whatever came next for his oldest friend.
